user_story_groups.story: As a local Codexter user, I can keep using normal Codex plus installed skills while the helper reads my filesystem ticket through one named adapter.
evidence: bin/codexter_boards.py normalizes ticket fields into WorkItem

user_story_groups.story: As a future Symphony caller, I need a stable WorkItem boundary so Symphony can launch Codex and Codexter can route skills/proof inside the workspace.
evidence: Codexter invocation remains diagnostic and does not launch Codex
BoardAdapter v1 is documented in docs/specs/board-compute-orchestration.md and skills/codexter-invocation/SKILL.md

user_story_groups.group: future Linear or Notion adapter author
user_story_groups.story: As an adapter author, I can see the methods and normalized shape I must implement without importing Ralph-specific behavior.
evidence: BoardAdapter Protocol exposes list_candidates, read_work_item, and write_evidence
FileTicketAdapter is the only live implementation and external adapters remain explicitly future work

user_story_groups.story: As a maintainer, I can verify path containment, source registry traceability, and no hidden board mutation.
evidence: write_evidence returns an explicit manual v1 result

risks.risk: Ralph still has its own selector logic.
risks.reason: TASK-0113 intentionally avoids destabilizing Ralph. A later ticket can reuse BoardAdapter after compute selector v2 and lease policy are clearer.

risks.risk: Filesystem evidence writeback is not automated.
risks.reason: Manual evidence writeback matches current Codexter ticket practice and avoids silent mutation until a traceable WriteResult update contract exists.

commands: python3 - <<'PY' ... feature registry status-aware validation ... PY
